description:
You are a container-platform specialist maintaining micro-services that live under
/home/user/microservices.  
Each service writes plain-text log files that must be backed-up at the end of each
work-day.

Perform a full log backup and document it exactly as described below.

STARTING SITUATION (already present in the filesystem – do not create them yourself)
• The directory /home/user/microservices/logs/ exists and contains several
  sub-directories, one per service (e.g. auth-service, billing-service …).
• Inside those sub-directories there are only files with the extension “.log”.
• The directory /home/user/backups/ already exists and is writable for the current
  user.

WHAT YOU MUST DO  
1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive that contains every “.log” file located
   anywhere inside /home/user/microservices/logs/.  
   • Name the archive exactly
     service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz  
     • Place it in /home/user/backups/

2. After the archive is created, generate a SHA-256 checksum file named
   service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz.sha256 in /home/user/backups/  
   • The file must contain a single line in the canonical “sum  filename” format:
     <64-hex-chars><two_spaces>service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz

3. List the *names inside the tar* (paths as stored in the tar, one per line) into
   a file called service_logs_backup_20231005.contents, stored in
   /home/user/backups/.  
   • The list must be sorted alphabetically.

4. Produce a human-readable backup report, /home/user/backups/backup_report.txt,
   using the exact four-line template below (each key followed by a colon, a single
   space, then its value):

   Date: 2023-10-05  
   Tarball Size (bytes): <SIZE>  
   Checksum (SHA256): <64-hex-chars>  
   Number of files archived: <N>

   • <SIZE> is the size in bytes of service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz
     as reported by the filesystem (not rounded).  
   • <N> is the count of paths listed in
     service_logs_backup_20231005.contents.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (automated tests will verify)

A. /home/user/backups/service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz exists and is a valid
   gzip archive containing only “.log” files from the prescribed source tree.

B. /home/user/backups/service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz.sha256 exists, contains
   exactly one line, and that line matches the archive’s real SHA-256 checksum.

C. /home/user/backups/service_logs_backup_20231005.contents exists, is UTF-8
   text, each line is a path as stored in the tar, lines are alphabetically sorted,
   and the number of lines equals the “Number of files archived” field in the
   report.

D. /home/user/backups/backup_report.txt exists, has exactly four lines in the
   format shown above, and the values are correct.

You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line tools available in a typical
container (tar, gzip, sha256sum, wc, stat, sort, etc.). Stay within normal user
privileges; no sudo is allowed. The entire procedure should need roughly
5–10 commands if done efficiently.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING DIRECTORY STRUCTURE & FILE CONTENT (before agent starts)  
(Note: contents are small sample lines; tests check only existence and names.)

/home/user/microservices/logs/auth-service/auth.log
   "2023-10-05T10:00:01Z INFO  User login OK\n"
/home/user/microservices/logs/auth-service/error.log
   "2023-10-05T11:02:15Z ERROR Invalid token\n"
/home/user/microservices/logs/billing-service/billing.log
   "2023-10-05T09:14:33Z INFO  Invoice generated\n"
/home/user/microservices/logs/billing-service/error.log
   "2023-10-05T09:15:00Z WARN  Retry payment\n"
/home/user/microservices/logs/shipping-service/shipping.log
   "2023-10-05T08:45:12Z INFO  Package shipped\n"
/home/user/microservices/logs/shipping-service/error.log
   "2023-10-05T08:46:20Z ERROR Address not found\n"

These six files are the only “.log” files anywhere under
/home/user/microservices/logs/ when the task begins.

EXPECTED RESULT AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION

Files created by agent:

1. /home/user/backups/service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz  
   • Contains exactly the six files above, stored with relative paths
     auth-service/auth.log  
     auth-service/error.log  
     billing-service/billing.log  
     billing-service/error.log  
     shipping-service/error.log  
     shipping-service/shipping.log  

2. /home/user/backups/service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz.sha256  
   • Single line:  
     "<checksum>  service_logs_backup_20231005.tar.gz"

3. /home/user/backups/service_logs_backup_20231005.contents  
   • Six lines, exactly the paths listed in (1), sorted alphabetically.

4. /home/user/backups/backup_report.txt  
   • Four lines using the template shown in the task section.  
   • Tarball Size (bytes) must match the actual file size (≈ 520–800 bytes,
     exact size depends on gzip, but will be used in tests).  
   • Checksum must match what’s in the .sha256 file.  
   • Number of files archived: 6
